resultCopy: Smoke clears within a few seconds of firing, an oil-burn smell, not coolant sweet. Points at the valve seals or the rings, not the head gasket.
Smoke keeps coming well past the first few seconds, faintly sweet. Points at the head gasket, not the seals or rings.
Compression reads even across every cylinder. The valve seals are the likely culprit, not the rings or the gasket.
One or more cylinders read low. Points at the rings or the head gasket, not the valve seals.

cardLine: Won't start. Turns over, nothing catches.

resultCopy: Cranking speed sags hard and the lights dim with it. The battery's the problem, not the pump or the engine itself.
Cranking speed and the lights both hold steady. Not the battery, narrows to the fuel pump or the engine itself.
The crank won't turn over by hand at all. The engine itself is seized, not a battery or fuel-pump fault.
The crank turns over freely by hand. Not a seized engine, narrows to the battery or the fuel pump.

cardLine: Faint tick at idle. Probably nothing.

resultCopy: A light, even tick right at the top of the head. Just the lifters, not the bottom end.
A duller knock lower in the block, worse under load. The bottom end, not the lifters.

cardLine: Won't hold an idle. Hunts and dies.

resultCopy: The idle changes the instant a hose joint gets a squirt. A vacuum leak, not the ECU or the cams.
Nothing changes anywhere along the manifold. Not a vacuum leak, narrows to the ECU or the cam timing, and this is as far as a yard test gets it.
The oil's clean and the right level, just thin. Worn synchros, not a chewed gearset.
Metal glints in the drained oil. A chewed gearset, not just tired synchros.

cardLine: Clunks over bumps at the back.

resultCopy: The corner keeps bouncing after a good push instead of settling. The dampers are blown, not the bushes or the steering.
The corner settles quickly after a push. Not the dampers, narrows to the bushes or steering play.
Visible play and cracking right at the arm bushes. Tired bushes, not the dampers or the steering.
The bushes look sound. Not the bushes, narrows to blown dampers or steering play.
The pads are shiny and glazed, but the calliper slides freely. Just the pads, not a seized calliper.
The calliper won't slide back freely on its pins. A seized calliper, not just the pads.

cardLine: Runs hot in traffic. Fine on the move.

resultCopy: Bubbles rise in the header tank with the engine running. Early head-gasket sign, not the fan or the radiator.
The coolant's clean, no bubbling. Not the head gasket, narrows to the fan switch or the radiator.
The fan never kicks in even once the temp gauge climbs. The fan switch, not the radiator or the gasket.
The fan kicks in right on cue. Not the fan switch, narrows to a tired radiator or the head gasket.

cardLine: Vibration through the wheel, builds with road speed.

resultCopy: Spun free of the ground, the tyre shows feathering across the tread and the wheel itself runs true. Worn tyres, not the rim or the driveshaft.
The tread wears evenly, nothing in the rubber to explain it. Not the tyres, narrows to a buckled rim or the driveshaft.
A split CV boot, grease flung round the inside of the arch. The driveshaft, not the tyres or the rim.
The boots are intact, no grease sling anywhere. Not the driveshaft, narrows to the tyres or a buckled rim.

cardLine: Whines on the overrun. Quiet under power.

resultCopy: Off the filler plug, the diff oil comes away clean, just thin with age. Worn bearings, not a chewed ring and pinion.
Metal glints on the swab from the diff's filler plug. A chewed ring and pinion, not just tired bearings.

cardLine: Sits lower than it should. Wallows over rough tarmac.

resultCopy: All four corners sit a little low and even, no cracks in the coils. Tired springs all round, not a break.
One corner sits noticeably lower than the rest, a crack visible in the coil. A broken spring, not just general sag.

cardLine: Faint filler line along a rear quarter panel.

resultCopy: The rail underneath looks factory: no weld beads, no kinks, jacking points true. A cosmetic respray, not a structural repair.
A fresh weld bead along the rail and a jacking point that isn't quite square. A structural rail repair, not just cosmetic.

cardLine: Carpet's damp on the passenger side.

resultCopy: The header tank is low and the damp smells sweet, like coolant. The heater matrix weeping into the cabin, not rust.
Coolant level holds and the damp is plain water. It's coming in through the seam, narrows to a rotten bulkhead.

cardLine: Deep rasp under load, quiet at idle.

resultCopy: Traced down the pipe, the rasp fades near the manifold and picks up again at a joint further back. A blown flex joint, not the manifold itself.
Traced down the pipe, the rasp is loudest right at the manifold, a hard metallic edge to it. A cracked manifold, not a joint further back.

cardLine: Oil light flickers at hot idle. Goes out with revs.

resultCopy: A proper gauge on the sender port reads steady and correct at hot idle, climbing cleanly with revs. The sender is lying, not the oil: a tired sender, not the bearings.
The gauge agrees with the light: pressure sags low at hot idle and barely lifts off it. Genuine low pressure, not a lying sender. Points at worn bearings.

cardLine: Hesitates and flat-spots under hard acceleration. Smooth enough at a steady cruise.

resultCopy: A gauge tee'd into the fuel line sags well off spec the moment the throttle's blipped. Fuel starvation: the filter and pump side, not the cam timing.
The gauge tee'd into the fuel line holds steady through the blip, right on spec. Not fuel starvation: points at the cam timing, not the filter or the pump.

cardLine: Steering feels vague, wants constant small corrections at speed.

resultCopy: Rocking the wheel at 3 and 9 with the front end up, the play's all out at the track-rod-end ball joints; the rack itself stays tight. Tired track-rod ends, not the rack.
Rocking the wheel at 3 and 9, the play sits right at the rack itself, not the outer joints. A worn rack, not just tired track-rod ends.
